checks.name: MCP authorization before persistence
checks.summary: Every tool resolves a server-configured principal and checks capability plus exact tenant and repository scope before a command reaches durable persistence.

checks.name: stable approval authority
checks.summary: Approval and rejection require an exact pending decision ID; generic run transitions cannot consume or bypass that decision.

checks.name: legacy HTTP authentication
checks.summary: The complete /v1 namespace fails closed behind a bounded static bearer authenticator, actor headers are ignored, and comparisons are constant-time.

checks.name: bearer transport protection
checks.summary: The CLI permits plaintext HTTP only to numeric loopback addresses, refuses remote plaintext endpoints, and never forwards credentials across redirects.

checks.name: migration and command concurrency
checks.summary: Fail-fast migration locks, stable lock ordering, optimistic versions, command barriers, and append-only evidence prevent split authority and unsafe downgrade.

residual_risks: Static bearer authentication is a local deployment primitive, not an internet-facing identity system; production remote access requires an authenticated TLS proxy or replacement transport identity.
The stdio MCP principal is deployment-configured and therefore inherits the security of the host process and client configuration.
Worker sandboxing, process-tree cancellation, and output secret redaction are intentionally deferred to Sprint 04.
